title: Happy Hour Crafts
description:
The Rebel Craft Collective
Wine + tasty snacks + your latest project and a friendly group of likeminded crafters... what better way to unwind? Come join us!

This is a relaxed, drop-in-friendly event. Come when you can, leave when you must. Newcomers and occasional-comers are always welcome!

**What do we mean by “crafts”?**
Whatever “craft” means to you! And whatever is portable enough to bring to a wine bar. Knitting, crochet, and other fiber arts are most common, but we have also warmly welcomed people who sketch, color, embroider, bead, and more. If your craft isn’t portable, we will happily ooh and aah over photos of your work-in-progress, or chat about your plans for your next project.

**Health & Safety**
Masks are very welcome, and nobody will pressure you to eat or drink. If the weather is nice, we will try to sit outside. If you aren’t eating/drinking during the event, please consider ordering some of La Dolce Vita’s delicious food to-go, in order to help support our gracious, long-time venue.

**Parking**
Parking in downtown Petaluma is free, but street parking has a strictly enforced 2 hour limit until 6pm. To park for longer, try the nearest garage:

* The Second Street Garage entrance is located on C Street between First and Second Streets. It's all day parking on every level and a short walk to La Dolce Vita.

**Ages**
It's a wine bar, so this event is for adult crafters (though you're certainly not required to drink wine!). We’re okay with babes-in-arms if you are, but this is not the right setting for wee crafters who are moving about on their own.

title: Opening Reception: Americana Reimagined and States of Anxiety, States of Hope works by Inez Storer
description:
Join us for the opening reception of PAC's Americana Reimagined exhibition and Inez Storer's States of Anxiety, States of Hope: Thursday, March 12, 5:30-7:30pmAdmission is Free but space is limited so reserve your ticket/s now before it is sold out.

About Americana Reimagined: On the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States, Americana Reimagined invited artists from across the greater Bay Area to explore, reinterpret, and reimagine the visual language of “Americana”. This juried exhibition looks beyond nostalgia to examine how familiar symbols, stories, and styles evolve over time—bridging the past and present through creativity and craft. 

“Americana” has long served as a reflection of everyday life and culture, from small-town iconography and handmade crafts to design movements and popular imagery. In Americana Reimagined artists reexamine this broad theme, juxtaposing the old and the new, and honoring timeless motifs while offering fresh perspectives on what they mean today.

Jurors 

Donna Seager, Donna Seager Fine Arts & Artists' Books  Richard Neill, Multimedia Artist and Film curator Simon Corwin, Petaluma Artist and Jewelry

States of Anxiety, States of HopeExhibition Dates: March 12 - April 18, 2026Opening Reception: Thursday, March 12, 5:30-7:30pm

About States of Anxiety, States of Hope: This exhibition presents a selection of works by artist Inez Storer that reflect on the emotional, cultural, and political complexities of contemporary life. Building on themes explored in Americana Reimagined, Storer's paintings offer a layered meditation on identity, memory, and collective experience in times of uncertainty and transformation.

Through her distinctive magical realist collage method, Storer constructs richly textured visual narratives that merge personal history with broader social realities. Her works are composed of fragments—symbols, images, and artifacts drawn from her life and interests.

Storer's paintings guide the viewer on a journey through both historical memory and internal landscapes. They balance darkness and light, anxiety and hope.  She asks us to reflect on the conditions of our moment. States of Anxiety, States of Hope affirms art's capacity to illuminate struggle while nurturing resilience, empathy, and connection.

title: States of Anxiety, States of Hope | Works of Inez Storer
description: [ { "type": "highlight", "id": "481f9063-c358-4f54-ba25-2bb0e1e78086", "shape": "underline", "isFront": false, "isAnimationEnabled": false, "animation": "draw", "duration": 0.5, "direction": "right", "color": { "type": "THEME_COLOR" }, "thickness": { "unit": "em", "value": 0.1 }, "linecap": "square" } ] States of Anxiety, States of Hope Exhibition Dates: March 12 - April 18, 2026 Opening Reception: Thursday, March 12, 5:30-7:30pm States of Anxiety, States of Hope  presents a selection of works by artist Inez Storer that reflect on the emotional, cultural, and political complexities of contemporary life. Building on themes explored in   Americana Reimagined ,  Storer’s paintings offer a layered meditation on identity, memory, and collective experience in times of uncertainty and transformation. Through her distinctive magical realist collage method, Storer constructs richly textured visual narratives that merge personal history with broader social realities. Her works are composed of fragments—symbols, images, and artifacts drawn from her life and interests. Storer's paintings guide the viewer on a journey through both historical memory and internal landscapes. They balance darkness and light, anxiety and hope.  She asks us to reflect on the conditions of our moment. States of Anxiety, States of Hope affirms art’s capacity to illuminate struggle while nurturing resilience, empathy, and connection. Biography Storer lives in Inverness, California, and has a studio in Point Reyes Station. Born in Santa Monica in 1933 to a Catholic father and Jewish mother, she spent time on Hollywood movie sets where her father worked as an art director. Influenced by stage design, her work often creates a sense of “theatrical space” and reflects her ancestry. Shortly before her death, her mother revealed that she had fled Germany to escape Nazism. Storer later learned of cousins who escaped on the Kindertransport. The frustration of being kept from the truth has infused her work with a distinct angst. Her work is held in many collections including the Jewish Museum in New York, Manetti Shrem Museum of Art at UC Davis, Oakland Museum of Art , the de Young Museum, San José Museum of Art, Monterey Museum of Art, Missoula Museum of Art, National Museum of American Jewish History, and the National Museum of Women in the Arts, Washington, DC. At the same time, our friends at IceHouse Gallery will hold a companion exhibition of Storer’s Works on Paper. Exhibition dates are March 7 - March 28. title: Crochet for Beginners and Beyond
description:
Schedule

Tuesdays, March 3 – 24, 2026 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Location

Room 201, Fiber Arts Studio

Experience Level

All Levels

Fiber Arts

Age\(s\)

18 and older

$80 – Registration CostLearn to crochet or expand your skills in four relaxed Tuesday evening sessions. Beginners master foundational stitches; experienced crafters get project guidance.

About the Class

Have you always wanted to learn to crochet – or are you ready to build on the skills you already have? Join us in the Fiber Arts Studio for four relaxed Tuesday evening sessions dedicated to learning and practicing crochet. Beginners will start with the essentials: how to hold the hook comfortably, maintain even tension, and work the foundational stitches – chain, single crochet, and double crochet. Students with prior experience are welcome to begin a new project or bring a work-in-progress for guidance and skill-building. All materials are provided for beginners. Returning or experienced crocheters may bring their own supplies or use yarn available in the studio.

title: Samba no Pé - First Class
description:
Get ready to feel the rhythm and energy of Rio de Janeiro! Samba no Pé \(Samba in the Foot\) is the captivating solo style of samba danced in the streets and at Carnival, known for its rapid, intricate footwork and infectious hip movements.

You will learn

The Basic Step: You will master the signature quick-quick-slow rhythm that defines Samba no Pé.

Arm & Hip Styling: You will learn how to add the authentic flair, movement, and energy that makes Brazilian samba so captivating.

Technique & Posture: You will receive tips on proper body alignment to help you dance longer, faster, and more safely.

Rhythm & Musicality: You will learn how to connect your movement directly to the irresistible beat of the bateria \(the samba drum section\).

This class is perfect for anyone looking to connect with the vibrant spirit of Brazil, improve coordination, and get a fantastic workout—all while learning the foundational steps of this iconic dance. No partner or prior dance experience is required! Just bring your enthusiasm and comfortable shoes!

Date & Time: Thursdays @6:30PM – 7:30PM

Address: 4140 Redwood Highway \#8 San Rafael, CA

Note: Arrive 10 minutes before. No floor marking shoe soles allowed.

title: Greg Lamboy - Live Every 2nd Thursday at Finnegan's Marin
description:
Greg Lamboy has called Finnegan's Marin home for over a decade. Every second Thursday of the month, this Tiburon-based troubadour settles into the warmly lit corner of Novato's beloved neighborhood pub and does what he does best: connects. An accomplished guitarist and award-winning songwriter, Lamboy has earned his stripes across the Bay Area scene both as a solo artist and as frontman of Pacific Standard, his soulful Americana band. His five acclaimed albums draw praise for melodies that are pitch-perfect and lyrics that feel like they were written specifically for you. At Finnegan's, a lively Irish pub and American bar and grill along a tree-lined street in downtown Novato, Lamboy's intimate style is perfectly at home. No pretense. No distance. Just a strong clear voice, commanding guitar, and songs that, as one critic put it, lift you above the everyday. Come early, grab a pint, and let the music find you. https://greglamboy.com/

*Every second Thursday of the month | Finnegan's Marin, 877 Grant Ave, Novato | Free*

title: Forró Dance with Eric and Abigail
description:
Get ready to connect, spin, and glide to the irresistible rhythms of Forró! Originating in the Northeast of Brazil, Forró is a captivating partner dance known for its close embrace, playful steps, and infectious, feel-good music. It is a social dance that will enrich your life, perfect for a night out or a casual gathering.

You Will Learn:

The Basic Step & Close Embrace: You will master the foundational quick-quick-slow rhythm and learn how to comfortably and confidently navigate the essential close-hold connection that defines Forró.

Leading & Following Techniques: Discover the clear, subtle body cues required to effortlessly lead your partner through turns and variations, and how to follow with grace and responsiveness.

Traveling & Stationary Patterns: Learn fundamental steps like the ‘Caminhada’ \(walking step\) and common stationary turns that will allow you to dance on any size floor.

Rhythm & Musicality: Connect your movement to the distinct musical styles of Forró—from the mellow ‘Xote’ to the faster, more energetic ‘Pé-de-Serra.’

This class is perfect for anyone looking to learn a social partner dance, connect with Brazilian culture, and enjoy a vibrant, community atmosphere. No partner or prior dance experience is required! Just bring your enthusiasm and comfortable shoes!

Class Details

Note: Arrive 10 minutes before. No floor marking shoe soles allowed.

Date & Time: Thursdays @7:30PM – 8:30PM

Address: 4140 Redwood Highway \#8 San Rafael, CA

description: Private Lives by Noël Coward March 6-21 TICKETS In Noël Coward's sparkling and sophisticated comedy of manners, Private Lives , a divorced couple, Amanda and Elyot, find themselves unexpectedly honeymooning at the same hotel with their new spouses. Their shared past ignites a firestorm of passion, leading them to abandon their new partners and escape together to Paris. This witty and chaotic escape reignites a turbulent romance filled with both adoration and dramatic clashes, as their spurned spouses, Victor and Sibyl, give chase. Discover this sharp, hilarious battle of the sexes, a timeless exploration of love, lust, and the scandalous, intoxicating nature of human relationships. Michael Fontaine, Stage Director

title: March Month of Flowers Challenge
description:
**Starting March 1st begins the "March Month of Flowers Challenge" (online).** 

You'll receive a list of prompts for every day in March. 

Create an art piece related to the prompt.

Share in our private Facebook group.

Be inspired and give inspiration to others.

**Only $5**, so everyone can join and share in this creative adventure!

***March Month of Flowers* Challenge** is here to give you a creative outlet, some structure, and respite from your brain. 

When you pick up a brush, pen, or pencil (thread, clay, fabric…), your brain gets a rest and becomes more alive and flexible. The world around you becomes more alive; you pick up on shades, details, and patterns all around you. Your intuition gets stronger. And fear lessens, anxiety fades, and curiosity grows. You stop worrying about making mistakes, and instead, you find the freedom to simply create

title: What About Age? Art Exhibit
description:
*What About Age?* art exhibit has arrived in Marin County! Originally displayed at NYU’s Kimmel Windows, this exhibit explores ageism through contemporary art, featuring works by both local youth and senior artists.

*What About Age?* is an art exhibit and educational initiative designed to challenge stereotypes about aging, address ageism and social isolation, and promote intergenerational dialogue through the lens of art.

In partnership with Youth in Arts, the exhibit features artworks by local youth and senior artists, showcasing how age intersects with creativity, identity, and experience. Under the guidance of Youth in Arts, these emerging and established artists work side by side to create collaborative pieces, fostering meaningful connections between generations and reflecting the exhibit’s core message: art is ageless.

The exhibit also includes artwork panels by New York-based writer and illustrator Aubrey Hirsch, first displayed at NYU’s Kimmel Windows and originally organized by Stacey Gordon, NYU’s Program Director of Next Phase Adult Caregiving and Retirement at Work Life.

The new exhibition will be on view at the Marin County Civic Center galleries from January 26 to May 6, 2026. Works will be displayed on the first and third floor exhibits, Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m.

The *What About Age?* art exhibit is presented by Marin Cultural Association in partnership with Marin Community Foundation and Youth in Arts. Beyond the art exhibit, the *What About Age?* initiative offers programming, led by Vivalon, designed to challenge ageism and provide resources for Marin County's growing aging population.

title: Big and Small; Pinching Pots with Dan Clauson
description:
Fridays, January 30 – March 13, 2026 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM

Experience Level

All Levels

Event Categories: Art Departments, Ceramics

Age\(s\)

17\+

$230 Registration Fee $30 Materials Fee $260 Total Registration Fee

A class that is all about pinching! With instructor Dan, students will learn handbuilding methods to coil and pitch their vessels. With specific direction in learning the techniques to build any size of pot, from a large scale vessel to a small cup. We will learn how to support scaling up our forms through drying methods, interior ‘bone’ structure, and mastering directionality with our hands. There will be a focus on how we each make a mark upon a pot, and how to do so with purpose. Each student will create work that reflects the time-honored nature of handbuilding; when intention is given to the silhouette of a form, the lip of a cup, and/or the comfort of a handle. Each class will involve a warmup, a demo, and worktime. Most classes will have \(fun&thoughtful\) homework.

title: Introduction to the Art of Bookmaking
description:
Schedule

Fridays, February 20 – March 13, 2026 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM

Experience Level

All Levels

Event Categories: Fiber Arts, Other, Printmaking

Age\(s\)

18 and older

$220 – Registration Cost $30 – Materials Fee $250 – Total Registration CostLearn essential bookbinding techniques in this 4-week introduction to book arts. Create zines, pamphlets, accordion folds, and more using traditional paper-folding and stitching methods.

What you’ll do

Join guest artist, Bettina Pauly in this incredible introduction to book arts! The art of bookmaking, otherwise known as bookbinding, is the physical craft of creating an artistic book from start to finish. In this introductory 4-session class, you will learn the basic use of bookbinding tools and materials, along with simple bookbinding structures that utilize paper softcovers. Students will learn several basic bookbinding structures and stitches that every beginning book artist should know! Experience technical skills such as paper folding, cutting, sewing and gluing, mixed with artistic elements of design to create functional, elegant objects built for storytelling. The instructor will offer guided instruction in a supportive learning environment with a limited class size. Each 3-hour session will meet for four consecutive weeks and focus on the following core bookbinding techniques:

Session 1: Intro to tools and paper folding; one sheet wonder zine, ‘pants’ fold with and without cover, map folds

Session 2: Pamphlets, and Japanese stab hole binding; three hole pamphlet stitch, five hole pamphlet stitch, Japanese stab binding two holes / five holes 

Session 3: Accordion folds; accordion fold, accordion fold tipped into envelope cover, accordion fold with non-adhesive cover

Session 4: Magic wallets, and personal projects

The class materials fee will include paper and all the tools needed to create each of these projects. Students will be able to take home all of their book projects at the end of the workshop. Please note: This class requires some hand dexterity to maneuver bookbinding tools. Participants should feel comfortable using tools with sharp edges such as an awl, x-acto knife, and scissors.

title: The Bonsai Show: Bonsai & Blossoms
description:
January 9–March 22, 2026

Step into a wonderland of blooming miniature trees to marvel at the living art of bonsai and the season’s first flowers. Then stroll through our Asian Woodland to admire majestic magnolias, cherries, and camellias in bloom, or join a tour or program to explore this timeless craft.

This year’s all-new presentation by the skilled artisans of the Redwood Empire Bonsai Society \(REBS\) highlights the grace and vitality of flowering bonsai—trees and shrubs trained to bloom in exquisite proportion to their diminutive scale. Delicate plum, azalea, camellia, cherry, and apricot bonsai take center stage, their blossoms appearing just as winter begins to yield to spring. These early bloomers symbolize hope and renewal—reminding us that beauty and resilience can emerge even in the coldest months.

The exhibition also draws inspiration from hanami, the centuries-old Japanese tradition of celebrating the fleeting beauty of blossoms. The Garden invites visitors to participate in this art of flower appreciation through Hanami Strolls—guided walks through the Garden’s Asian woodland where early-blooming magnolias, cherries, and camellias offer their own ephemeral display.

title: Art Exhibit: Celebrating Women
description:
Date: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE  2/13/26

**MARCH EXHIBIT: CELEBRATING WOMEN**

EXHIBIT DATES:  February 23 – March 29, 2026

**RECEPTION: SATURDAY, MARCH 7, 2026, 4-6 pm: Join in to meet the artists and celebrate the show. Take part in the Gallery’s free monthly drawing for a beautiful work by a Gallery artist.  Refreshments will be served.**

Sonoma County artist, **Tosya Shore,** presents **Celebrating Women,** paintings that commemorate women in honor of Women’s History Month. Shore’s watercolor works capture women in their element, showing the multiple facets of their lives.  These dynamic paintings break away with strong shapes and interlacing patterns that draw the viewer in exciting ways.  A la Degas, we catch a ballet dancer lacing her shoes in “Good Point.”  The artist takes us into nature with “Redwood Rendezvous” where dominant trunk shapes frame a couple enjoying a glorious forest scene.    “Just Back from Vegas” captures a fleeting view of elegance as the woman in question catches her breath.  “There’s such special feeling in these everyday moments, I love to turn the mundane into magic – women deserve nothing less,” noted Shore. 

Meet the artist: Shore will be hosting the Gallery on March 5 and 19 from 11am to 6 pm.

 The Stairway Smallworks Showcase features **Donna Schaffer’s Ooh La La, Plein Air,** a collection unveiling oil paintings made on her recent trip to France.  Roaming the streets of Paris, toting paints and palette, Schaffer captured iconic scenes buzzing with “la belle vie.” The viewer catches a true cultural vibe in “Café le Bistro”, the site of a favored hangout for espresso and conversation.  One is awed by the structure rising from the ground-level trees in Schaffer’s unique view of the Eiffel Tower in “Paris is for Lovers.” “Every day was an adventure in Paris – I hope through these plein air vignettes, to share the excitement of being there”, stated Schaffer.

Meet the artist: Schaffer will be hosting the Gallery on March 21 and 22 from 11am to 6 pm.

Upstairs Art Gallery, 306 Center Street, Healdsburg, CA 95448. (707) 431-4214.: OPEN DAILY: 11am – 6 pm.    **www.upstairsart gallery.net** 

For further information: Contact: Beverly Bird, Press Contact, at bvdrio@sonic.net or 707-799-1490.

Immerse yourself in the world of wine as you stroll through our chardonnay vineyard, rooted in six generations of farming legacy in the heart of the Russian River Valley. Witness firsthand the art and passion behind our winemaking process with an exclusive tour of our modest crush pad and barrel room. Relish with a tasting of five current releases and a special library wine, accompanied with a curated cheese and charcuterie board, and finished with decadent artisanal chocolates. title: Maija Peeples-Bright: Life is Just a Bowl of Terriers
description: Maija Peeples-Bright is one of the few women artists associated with Northern California's Funk Art movement of the late 1960s and '70s. Peeples-Bright's wildly inventive practice comprises robust compositions bustling with animals of all kinds-her "beasties" as she calls them-forming surreal, imaginative landscapes in audacious impasto. Her zoological arrangements represent alternate realities, infusing exuberance and a sense of the absurd into impossible scenarios. Highly distinctive, her textural creature patterns revel in the extreme, often taking up every inch of the canvas, multiplying her beasties into dizzying arrangements. Peeples-Bright invites us into a decadence-gone-wild world all her own. The exhibition will feature a retrospective look at Peeples-Bright's career, from her early days of the Funk Movement to today.

title: Adult Gymnastics Class
description:
We’re offering Adult Gymnastics Classes for the 18\+ crowd!

Adult Gymnastics classes occur every Tuesday 5:00 PM to 6:30 PM & Friday from 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM.

$20 per person, OR pay for a whole 8-week session in advance through our site. You can attend on Tuesday, Friday, or both!

Please bring the class fee in cash at the time of arrival. This can be a DROP IN, so there is no need to sign up online before coming!

If you’ve ever wanted to try out gymnastics or get back into it in adulthood, now is your chance! During our adult classes, open to all genders, our coaches will move at the pace of each student, helping them achieve goals they set for themselves. No experience is necessary to be involved in this class, so this is a great opportunity to learn the basics and get some exercise, no matter what age you are! Try out our trampoline, bars, beam, and other gym equipment in a safe and supportive environment. You can build strength and increase your range of motion while having fun like you’re a kid again.

Ask our coaches what you can work on between classes, and they’ll help personalize your training!

Please wear something comfortable and keep long hair tied back. Skirts and restrictive clothing are not recommended. You must remove your shoes before going onto the mat or using equipment. Please let us know if you have any physical limitations or health conditions that may interfere with doing certain motions. We recommend bringing a water bottle.

You can email us for more info or join our mailing list at: info@sonomagymnasticsacademy.com

title: Online: Resilience & the Open Heart: Somatic Practices for Everyday Stress
description:
**with Pawan Bareja, PhD**

When we are out of alignment with our deepest heart values, life feels stressful. Whether the trigger is personal heartbreak, economic pressure, relational conflict, or the weight of the climate crisis, the body often reacts with anxiety, collapse, anger, or overwhelm long before the mind can make sense of it. You are invited to move from stress to love by transforming these patterns through embodied heart practices.

Each week, we will begin by gently noticing where activation lives in the body. We’ll then engage in embodied heart-opening practices to soothe the nervous system and reconnect with inner clarity. After practice, we will re-check the nervous system to observe what has shifted, revealing resilience as something softly felt, measurable, and trainable.

As the heart opens and the body softens, many experience deeper rest, clearer thinking, a renewed sense of connection to loved ones, and the courage to live a more meaningful life.

The fiber artists include Britt-Marie Alm, Stephanie Brown, Risa Iwasaki Culbertson, Amanda Farrell, Jennifer Laursen, Youngmi Angela Pak, Leigh Ann Parente and Vida Vazquez who each create, in unique ways, something new from raw or recycled materials, intuitively and humanely imbuing them with beauty and authenticity.

description:
Ready to learn intermediate steps and technique?

Are you a salsa dancer who has the basics down but is ready to add more? Our Intermediate Salsa Class with Tyler is designed to help you transform your dancing moves. In this class, you’ll go beyond the basics to learn intricate footwork, enhance your leading and following skills, and master the body movement that will make you shine on the dance floor. This class is for anyone ready to learn the techniques and intermediate steps needed to take their dancing to the next level.

You’ll learn intermediate dancing techniques that will help you:

Express yourself with sharper footwork and styling.

Master complex turn patterns and combinations.

Strengthen your lead and follow for seamless, connected dancing.

Adapt to different partners and music with ease on the social dance floor.

What to Expect: Instructor Tyler blends a fun, supportive atmosphere with structured lessons, so you’re always challenged but never overwhelmed. Helping you progress steadily while keeping the joy of salsa front and center.

Details:

No partner necessary—rotations ensure everyone dances.

Please wear non-marking shoes to protect the floor.

Arrive 10 minutes early to warm up and get settled.

When: Fridays, 6:30 PM – 7:30 PM

Instructor: Tyler Crandall

Where: 4140 Redwood Highway \#8, San Rafael, CA

Hilarious or sublime, poignant and personal tales told through the ancient oral tradition of storytelling, this evening will be sure to move, delight & intrigue you. This is an event for mature audiences & not intended for children. Interested storytellers will have a chance to throw their name in the hat to present their story. Remember no notes & 7 minutes max! $10 GA, $5 for OCA members. Pay at the door. Storytellers will be selected randomly from willing participants.

description: Osito was formed in the summer of 2015 in Santa Rosa, CA during a production of the hit musical “Buddy” the Buddy Holly story. The group immediately had too much fun playing music across California and Texas, and over the next decade couldn’t quit finding new incarnations of the same songwriter-driven, rock-n-roll roots that connected them in the first place. Today, their balance of in-the-pocket guitar-forward compositions and deep-cut, horn-centric grooves continues to offer a versatility of instrumentation unique to the Bay Area scene.

title: The Savannah Sipping Society; a Jones Hope Wooten Comedy
description:
**Four Southern women turn life’s curve balls into an uproarious, heartwarming journey of friendship, laughter, and fresh starts.**

In this delightful, laugh-a-minute comedy, four unique Southern women, all needing to escape the sameness of their day-to-day routines, are drawn together by Fate—and an impromptu happy hour—and decide it’s high time to reclaim the enthusiasm for life they’ve lost through the years. Over the course of six months, filled with laughter, hilarious misadventures, and the occasional liquid refreshment, these middle-aged women successfully bond and find the confidence to jump start their new lives. Together, they discover lasting friendships and a renewed determination to live in the moment—and most importantly, realize it’s never too late to make new old friends.

title: The Seasons - Dance on Ice
description:
Join us at Snoopy's Home Ice for an evening of art and athleticism of Dance on Ice.

Snoopy's Home Ice

March 13th 2026 at 7PM

Olympic Champion Gabriella Papadakis leads Ice Dance International's new tour, THE SEASONS—a celebration of artistry, athleticism, and speed that turns the rink into a living stage. Papadakis joins 2-time United States Champion Alissa Czisny and skating artist Rohene Ward in a company of ten world class skaters. From the opening movement they shape formations that move through four acts; each sharing a story that takes the audience on a journey.

THE SEASONS bursts with color, emotion, and momentum.  Ice Dance International company members launch daring lifts and jumps, crisp footwork, and seamless transitions that thrilled audiences on IDI's 2025 tour.

Papadakis, Czisny and Ward's presence elevates every phrase—their power, musical instinct, and magnetic connection ignite the audience and sharpen the edge of every moment. This is skating unbound: athletic, cinematic, and wildly alive.

Reaching 14 cities in early 2026, this is the rare chance to witness the possibilities of performance dance on ice. IDI's Artistic Director Douglas Webster says “THE SEASONS isn't just a show—it's a pulse, a spark, a reminder that when blades meet music and movement meets heart, the ice itself begins to sing.” 

Edge Class details: The edge class are meant to be community events that allow an opportunity to skate and warm up with the IDI company. Skills required are a solid ability of forwards and backwards skating/cross overs, all basic edges and turns through forward and backward 3s. Classes will develop past 3 turns into more advanced exercises. Both youth and adults are welcome. These classes are not learn to skate classes and advance at a rapid pace.

Meet and Greets follow the show and are a chance for VIP ticket holders to get autographs and photos with the IDI cast members. It generally take the cast 25 minutes to finish a show, regroup and get to the meet and greet! There will be flyers and markers for signing.

title: Indie After Dark
description:
Indie After Dark – Vallejo’s Night for Original Music

September 12 performers:

🔥 Agness Twin

San Francisco’s hypnotic post-punk / indie project led by Elisa Ovrahim. Gorgeous layered vocals, ringing guitars, cinematic folk shadows, and minimalist ethereal pop. Their new album Turn To Gold \(2024\) was mastered by John Golden \(Soundgarden, Melvins\) and has already carved its place on indie radio. Fresh from the Love & Rockets tour, Agness Twin’s live set will swallow you whole.

🔥 Pro-Pain!

Bay Area 4-piece that’s “HAVING A BLAST.” High-voltage energy, pure grit, and a sound built to light up the room. With their recent Bombs Away video, they’re proof that Vallejo’s underground scene is very much alive and screaming.

Every Second Friday, Indie After Dark brings the spotlight to independent music at the intimate and vibey 4th Corner Speakeasy in downtown Vallejo. Just steps from the heart of the Vallejo Art Walk, this monthly series invites you to discover bold new sounds in a setting made for connection—where the lights are low, the cocktails are strong, and the music runs deep.

Come early for the art, stay late for the music. It’s a night where Vallejo’s creative soul shines.

title: Private Lives
location: Mercury Theater, 3333 Petaluma Blvd N, Petaluma, CA 94952
description: Private Lives by Noël Coward March 6-21 TICKETS In Noël Coward's sparkling and sophisticated comedy of manners, Private Lives , a divorced couple, Amanda and Elyot, find themselves unexpectedly honeymooning at the same hotel with their new spouses. Their shared past ignites a firestorm of passion, leading them to abandon their new partners and escape together to Paris. This witty and chaotic escape reignites a turbulent romance filled with both adoration and dramatic clashes, as their spurned spouses, Victor and Sibyl, give chase. Discover this sharp, hilarious battle of the sexes, a timeless exploration of love, lust, and the scandalous, intoxicating nature of human relationships. Michael Fontaine, Stage Director

title: Safari West Presents! Wildlands Network
description: Conservation Dinner Series: Mari Galloway of  Wildlands Network joins us in the elephant room after dinner to give a talk about protecting local ecosystems, advancing Innovative Policy, transforming transportation networks, and building community. The conservation dinner series is complimentary to all dinner guests. If you want to attend this presentation, please call our reservations team at \(707\) 566-3667 or \(800\) 616-2695 and they can book it for you. Dinner starts at 7:00 PM.    Speaker Bio: As California Program Manager for Wildlands Network, Mari develops collaborative and strategic partnerships to further policy, road ecology, and land use initiatives that promote wildlife corridors and core habitat protections throughout the State of California. Mari spent her childhood on a farm in the San Joaquin Valley of California and her summers in the Sierra Nevada, near Ebbetts Pass. Her outdoor exposure inspired her to earn her bachelor's degree in environmental science from Humboldt State University. After college, she rode her bike across the U.S. and lived off the grid in Hawaii before taking a job coordinating settlements at a personal injury law firm in downtown San Francisco to gain exposure to the practice of law. In 2017, she attended the University of Oregon and obtained her law degree in 2020 and became licensed to practice law in both Oregon and California the following year. Mari lives in the San Joaquin Valley. When not strategizing a connected California, you'll find her with her dog, Bodie, either backpacking, trail running, cooking, practicing piano, brewing beer, gardening, or sketching.

title: March Month of Flowers Challenge
description:
**Starting March 1st begins the "March Month of Flowers Challenge" (online).** 

You'll receive a list of prompts for every day in March. 

Create an art piece related to the prompt.

Share in our private Facebook group.

Be inspired and give inspiration to others.

**Only $5**, so everyone can join and share in this creative adventure!

***March Month of Flowers* Challenge** is here to give you a creative outlet, some structure, and respite from your brain. 

When you pick up a brush, pen, or pencil (thread, clay, fabric…), your brain gets a rest and becomes more alive and flexible. The world around you becomes more alive; you pick up on shades, details, and patterns all around you. Your intuition gets stronger. And fear lessens, anxiety fades, and curiosity grows. You stop worrying about making mistakes, and instead, you find the freedom to simply create

title: Medic First Aid CPR and AED Training
description: Course Description: This course goes a step beyond basic CPR. In this four-hour class, students will learn information and skills to provide effective emergency care to an injured or ill person before first responders arrive. The workplace is the focus, using industrial and office settings for the scenario-based learning environment. CPR, plus other lifesaving skills including bleeding control, choke saving, hazardous conditions awareness, and an introduction to the AED. Each students receives a manual, instruction, mannequin use and Medic First Aid card valid for two years. Course Audience: The class-of-choice for non-medical people, this class is the perfect course for any individual who would like to be prepared to respond to a cardiac emergency, anytime and anywhere. Great for on-site training for employees who have a duty to respond to a cardiac emergency due to job responsibilities or regulatory requirements.

title: Valentine Vista at Moore Creek Park
description:
Go Wild Hikers
Join me in exploring Moore Creek Park's 1,600 acres of rugged terrain including Oak woodlands, Douglas Fir & Madrone forest, grasslands, chaparral and spectacular wildflowers (in season). Wildlife in the park is plentiful, including large mammals such as black bears, mountain lions, bobcat, gray fox and coyote. Best for individuals with at least a moderate level of fitness.

**Where We Meet/Important Directions**
Moore Creek Park
2602 Chiles Pope Valley Rd, St Helena, CA 94574
**Get there Early! Carpool if you can - This is a very Popular Park!**
South on Hwy 128 past Calistoga - Left on Deer Park Rd (Sign Pacific Union College) - Right on Silverado Trail - Left on Sage Valley Rd/Hwy 128 - Past Lake Hennessey - Pay Attention - Left on Chiles Pope Valley Rd - Left into Moore Creek Park past a private house on a very narrow road there is a large parking lot.
Give yourself plenty of time to arrive. It's often farther than you think.

**The Hike (group hike #335**
Valentine Vista and Moore Creek trails - A loop hike
Distance - 4.75 miles/7.64 kilometers
Elevation change - 800 feet/244 meters
Difficulty - 6 out of 10 - Moderately easy
Exposures - 60% sun/40 % shade - poison oak, ticks/insects and views.
Must be sure footed (confident and competent)!
Bring plenty of water. At least 34 oz./1 liter!
Dogs - No. They are prohibited in Moore Crk (This Hike) and permitted at the Lake Hennessey section (other hikes).
Facilities at trail head - Port O Potti.
I suggest you stop elsewhere.
Cost - $5 cash.
Parking - Free - Napa Ag & Open Space excepts donations.
Remember to wear appropriate hiking shoes, bring plenty of water, a hat, a snack/lunch and layered clothing.
Please Do Not Use Any scented products the day of a group hike!
Of course we will all look out for each other but ultimately, hikers are responsible for their own safety and well-being during hikes.

**After Hike Gathering?**
Bella Bakery & Cafe
1353 Lincoln Avenue Calistoga, CA 94515
Monday-Saturday: 6am-2pm
Sunday: 7am-2pm
707-942-1443
bellabakerycakes@gmail.com

**When We Meet**
Saturday March 14, 2026
830 am - 1130 am
**Get there Early! Carpool if you can - This is a very Popular Park!**
If your plans change after you sign up even if you're on the waitlist, please update your RSVP.
Do Not Attend if you are sick, running a fever, were exposed to or have tested positive for Covid-19 or any other contagion!
Hike may be postponed or cancelled in case of excessive heat, rain,
smoke/AQI, illness, zombie apocalypse etc... Check your email the
night before for any possible changes.

**Notice to New Hikers Only**
**When a New Hiker (never hiked with me before) doesn't show up (No Show) to their first hike with the group, they will be removed (not banned) from the group immediately.**

title: Bachata Beginners Saturday
description:
This class is perfect for bachata beginners that want to learn basic steps and skills, or those who want to refresh their foundational skills. We understand that learning something new can feel a little intimidating. That’s why our supportive and relaxed environment is specifically designed to make learning bachata basics an absolute blast!

Our friendly instructors will guide you through:

Basic directional steps: You’ll learn the fundamental movements that form the building blocks of all bachata dancing.

Proper frame for partner work: Discover how to connect comfortably and effectively with a partner, setting you up for smooth and enjoyable dances.

Simple turn patterns: We’ll introduce you to easy-to-follow turn patterns that will have you dancing with a partner in no time.

We’ve carefully organized our curriculum to ensure that each movement is easy to grasp and remember, building your confidence with every step. Our goal is to make learning fun. Join us to learn Bachata in Marin.

Important Details

No dance experience required: Whether you’ve never danced before or it’s been a while, you’re welcome here!

No partner necessary: Come solo or bring a friend—either way, you’ll have plenty of opportunities to dance.

Please wear non-marking shoe soles: This helps us keep our dance floor in great condition.

Come join us and discover the joy of bachata! We can’t wait to see you on the dance floor.

Address: 4140 Redwood Highway \#8 San Rafael, CA

title: BLS Certification Class (American Heart Association)
description:
BLS Certification Class \(AHA\) - In Person, Instructor-Led

American Heart Association BLS certification class \(in-person, instructor-led class in our Rohnert Park classroom\). BLS \(Basic Life Support\) is the required CPR class for healthcare providers. Includes cardiac and respiratory arrest management, CPR, AED use, and choking treatment for all ages. This class is appropriate for initial BLS or BLS renewal.

Vive CPR is an American Heart Association \(AHA\) Training Site. AHA "BLS Provider" certifications are issued on the same day.

We also offer Blended Learning BLS certification courses \(online course plus in-person Skills Session\).

Learn from a top-rated, local CPR training company with over 900 5-star reviews! Our classes are fun, engaging, and expertly taught by experienced first responders.

Learn More & Enroll: https://vivecpr.com/class/bls-certification/

Questions? Call/text \(707\) 612-3277 or https://vivecpr.com/contact

title: Overeaters Anonymous (OA) Newcomer Meeting
description:
**Is Food a Problem for You? You Are Not Alone.**

Overeaters Anonymous (OA) is a fellowship of people who support each other in recovering from compulsive eating and food behaviors. If you feel you have a problem with food, OA welcomes you—no matter what your background, beliefs, or eating habits. 

We hold an in-person newcomer meeting on first, second and fourth **Saturdays of the month from 9:45 - 10:15 AM at** ​**Unitarian Universalist Congregation of Marin, 240 Channing Way, San Rafael CA 94903**. Contact: Sue M. 415-298-6792​. You may view all North Bay OA in-person and online meetings at https://www.northbayoa.org/meetings.html

In OA, members experience **spiritual, emotional, and physical recovery** by practicing the **Twelve Steps** and living by their spiritual principles—one day at a time.

OA is **not** a diet club or calorie-counting program.

Members have different plans of eating.

OA does **not** endorse any specific food plan. 

If food is causing problems in your life, you don’t have to struggle alone. Recovery is possible, and help is available.

**Visit:** https://www.northbayoa.org/

**Find a meeting:** https://www.northbayoa.org/meetings.html

title: Aligning with Our Deepest Knowing: Wise View on the Path of Awakening
description:
with Dana DePalma, MA, MFT and Matthew Brensilver, PhD

The Buddha says that wise view is the starting point—it is also the culmination of awakening. Yet, delusion comes camouflaged as wisdom. What we do not yet understand shapes our view and obscures freedom. What are the processes by which we realize and align with our deepest knowing?

Together, we’ll consider the various aspects of the Dharma path that help us wake up to the assumptions that shape our experience and behavior: deep attention, spiritual friendship, and surrendering to this moment. We’ll reflect on what we’ve known in our clearest moments and what truly matters in how we relate to life. Together we’ll open to our embodied experience of wise view and how it supports meeting each moment with wisdom and love.

During this day of practice, we’ll offer guided and silent meditation practice, Dharma reflections, and time for dialogue. All are welcome.

title: Habitat Hikes: Chaparral
description:
Join CA Naturalist and Sugarloaf docent Marie Kay Hansen through the featured habitat: chaparral. Learn about the largest native plant community flora in California, characterized by rugged terrain and adaptive flora, during this 2-mile hike along Goodspeed Trail up to Pierson Rd, possibly until the unnamed creek crossing, and back. This hike is the first in our four-part "Habitats of Sugarloaf" hike series. Other hikes will explore the oak woodland, meadow and redwood forest habitats. 

Meet at the Goodspeed parking lot. Tickets are $10 for general admission, $5 for youth (12-17 year olds; all minors must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian), students, Sugarloaf members, Sugarloaf or RFO volunteers, and free for children under 12. Wear layers, appropriate footwear for muddy and/or slippery trails, bring at least a quart of water, sun protection, and if desired, a snack, and binoculars. Heavy rain cancels. Parking fees apply.

$265 – Registration Cost $30 – Materials Fee $295 – Total Registration CostLearn photo transfer techniques onto canvas, wood, glass & paper in this hands-on workshop with Bay Area artist Margot Hartford. Create unique art pieces!

About the Class

Join Bay Area guest artist Margot Hartford, in this fun and creative one-day workshop where students will explore a unique photo transfer technique using personalized images. Learn how to effortlessly transfer photos onto various surfaces, including paper, canvas, glass and wood. Margot will demonstrate this process using a printable clear acetate, a specialized medium, and acrylic paint. Students will be introduced to the technique and will have the hands-on opportunity to create. The instructor will offer guided instruction in a supportive learning environment with a limited class size.

Workshop Requirements

After registration, in advance of the workshop, students will be required to email eight images to the instructor so they will be ready for use during the session. Each student will receive paper and wood panels to use as surfaces for transferring their images. The class materials fee will include all the tools needed to create the project. Students will be able to take home all of their images and finished projects at the end of the workshop.

Additional Notice

This class requires hand dexterity to maneuver tools, including X-Acto blades, and paintbrushes. The workshop includes six hours of instruction with a short 30 minute lunch break.All Silver Memberships and above receive 10% off the registration cost for any class purchase over $40. Materials fees are not discounted.

Become a member today and find community, connection, and creativity.

title: Italian Ricotta Favorites: Sweet and Savory Classics
description:
Schedule

Saturday, March 14, 2026 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM

Experience Level

All Levels

Bilingual, Culinary Arts

Age\(s\)

18 and older

$100 – Registration CostExplore ricotta three ways in this Italian cooking class. Make delicate gnudi, whipped ricotta crostini, and traditional torta di ricotta romana.

What you’ll do

Discover the incredible versatility of ricotta cheese in this hands-on Italian cooking class featuring three distinct preparations that showcase this beloved ingredient. You’ll learn techniques ranging from delicate pasta-making to elegant appetizers and classic Italian desserts. Start by creating pillowy ricotta gnudi, those cloud-like dumplings that are lighter than traditional gnocchi, served with a vibrant tomato sauce. Master the art of achieving the perfect texture and learn how to handle the delicate dough for consistently beautiful results. Next, prepare whipped ricotta crostini topped with honey and fresh thyme, an elegant appetizer that balances creamy, sweet, and herbaceous flavors on crispy bread. Finally, you’ll bake torta di ricotta romana, a traditional Roman ricotta cake that’s subtly sweet and wonderfully moist. This classic dessert demonstrates how ricotta creates tender, delicate baked goods with authentic Italian character. Throughout the class, you’ll gain confidence working with fresh ricotta, understanding how its moisture content and handling affect each dish differently. Whether you’re looking to expand your Italian cooking repertoire or simply love ricotta, this class provides techniques and recipes you’ll return to again and again. Perfect for intermediate home cooks ready to explore authentic Italian preparations.Membership has its benefits

All Silver Memberships and above receive 10% off the registration cost for any class purchase over $40. Materials fees are not discounted.

Become a member today and find community, connection, and creativity.

title: Salsa Beginner Marin – Saturday
description:
**Ready to learn intermediate steps and technique?** Join on on Saturday – Salsa Beginner Marin

Are you a beginner who’s always wanted to learn salsa? Salsa classes are designed to help you build a strong foundation. In this class, you’ll learn the fundamental footwork, practice the basic turns, and understand the essential body movement that will make you feel confident on the dance floor. This class is perfect for anyone ready to learn the techniques and foundational steps needed to start their salsa journey.

Salsa is fun, and we make learning it stress-free! Our relaxed, beginner-friendly class is the ideal starting point. You don’t need a partner, nor any dance experience!

In this class you will learn:

Basic directional steps that form the foundation of salsa.

Proper frame for comfortable and effective partner-work.

Simple, easy-to-remember turn patterns.

Get ready to learn the fundamentals and have a great time!

**Details:**

**No partner necessary**—rotations ensure everyone dances

Please wear **non-marking shoes** to protect the floor

Arrive 10 minutes early; warm up and settle in

**When:** Saturday, 10:00 am – 11:00 am

**Where:** 4140 Redwood Highway #8, San Rafael, CA

title: San Geronimo Repair Fair + Clothing Swap
description:
Do you have a lamp, small appliance, textile, or bike in need of repair?

Bring it to the Repair Fair!

Volunteers and specialized repair professionals will be on-hand to repair\* your items.  We ask that you stay with your item while it's being fixed, so you can learn tips for future maintenance, how to shop for repairable goods, and even how to repair things yourself!  

If you’d like to bring an item to repair, please register the item here: https://www.reusealliance.org/rf-reg \(Pre-registering allows us to prepare for your item!\)

This is a free public event.

This event will also include a Clothing Swap. Please bring clothing items and accessories \(clean and in good shape\) to share and bring home something new to you!

\*We cannot guarantee your item\(s\) will be repaired, depending on the existing damage or breakage, ability of volunteer fixers, and availability of materials for repair. Note we cannot work on microwaves and we can’t repair cell phones but we’re happy to refer you to local professionals who can. In addition, the textile department is not able to perform the following:

Replacing zippers

Repairing shoes, leather, or vinyl materials

Performing alterations \(making garments bigger/smaller\)

Replacing rivets/grommets

Hemming certain fabrics \(stretchy fabric, sleek/luxury fabrics e.g. prom dresses\)

If you are in need of garment hemming, we require that you please pin your garment to the desired length before you arrive.

Join Us!

Does fixing things sound like a fun way to spend a Saturday afternoon?  Or want to help out in other ways?  We need you!

Email volunteer@reusealliance.org or sign up here: https://volunteersignup.org/RMHLA

title: The Bonsai Show: Bonsai & Blossoms
description:
January 9–March 22, 2026

Step into a wonderland of blooming miniature trees to marvel at the living art of bonsai and the season’s first flowers. Then stroll through our Asian Woodland to admire majestic magnolias, cherries, and camellias in bloom, or join a tour or program to explore this timeless craft.

This year’s all-new presentation by the skilled artisans of the Redwood Empire Bonsai Society \(REBS\) highlights the grace and vitality of flowering bonsai—trees and shrubs trained to bloom in exquisite proportion to their diminutive scale. Delicate plum, azalea, camellia, cherry, and apricot bonsai take center stage, their blossoms appearing just as winter begins to yield to spring. These early bloomers symbolize hope and renewal—reminding us that beauty and resilience can emerge even in the coldest months.

The exhibition also draws inspiration from hanami, the centuries-old Japanese tradition of celebrating the fleeting beauty of blossoms. The Garden invites visitors to participate in this art of flower appreciation through Hanami Strolls—guided walks through the Garden’s Asian woodland where early-blooming magnolias, cherries, and camellias offer their own ephemeral display.

title: The Journey: A Painter's Narrative

Designed for all experience levels, the fair highlights sustainable gardening practices, environmental stewardship, food growing, and outdoor wellness. Attendees can explore a variety of vendor tables offering free educational materials, resources, seeds, plants, tools, and other garden-related giveaways. Many vendors will also feature fun, hands-on activities and interactive items that make gardening accessible and engaging for all ages. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just getting started, the Vallejo Gardening Fair is a welcoming space to discover local resources, connect with community experts, and leave inspired to grow-together.

title: Art Exhibit: Celebrating Women
description:
Date: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE  2/13/26

**MARCH EXHIBIT: CELEBRATING WOMEN**

EXHIBIT DATES:  February 23 – March 29, 2026

**RECEPTION: SATURDAY, MARCH 7, 2026, 4-6 pm: Join in to meet the artists and celebrate the show. Take part in the Gallery’s free monthly drawing for a beautiful work by a Gallery artist.  Refreshments will be served.**

Sonoma County artist, **Tosya Shore,** presents **Celebrating Women,** paintings that commemorate women in honor of Women’s History Month. Shore’s watercolor works capture women in their element, showing the multiple facets of their lives.  These dynamic paintings break away with strong shapes and interlacing patterns that draw the viewer in exciting ways.  A la Degas, we catch a ballet dancer lacing her shoes in “Good Point.”  The artist takes us into nature with “Redwood Rendezvous” where dominant trunk shapes frame a couple enjoying a glorious forest scene.    “Just Back from Vegas” captures a fleeting view of elegance as the woman in question catches her breath.  “There’s such special feeling in these everyday moments, I love to turn the mundane into magic – women deserve nothing less,” noted Shore. 

Meet the artist: Shore will be hosting the Gallery on March 5 and 19 from 11am to 6 pm.

 The Stairway Smallworks Showcase features **Donna Schaffer’s Ooh La La, Plein Air,** a collection unveiling oil paintings made on her recent trip to France.  Roaming the streets of Paris, toting paints and palette, Schaffer captured iconic scenes buzzing with “la belle vie.” The viewer catches a true cultural vibe in “Café le Bistro”, the site of a favored hangout for espresso and conversation.  One is awed by the structure rising from the ground-level trees in Schaffer’s unique view of the Eiffel Tower in “Paris is for Lovers.” “Every day was an adventure in Paris – I hope through these plein air vignettes, to share the excitement of being there”, stated Schaffer.

Meet the artist: Schaffer will be hosting the Gallery on March 21 and 22 from 11am to 6 pm.

Upstairs Art Gallery, 306 Center Street, Healdsburg, CA 95448. (707) 431-4214.: OPEN DAILY: 11am – 6 pm.    **www.upstairsart gallery.net** 

For further information: Contact: Beverly Bird, Press Contact, at bvdrio@sonic.net or 707-799-1490.

Registration Fee includes full enrollment for 9 weeks of instruction and the recital costume fee

A fun and positive environment where kids can learn beginning ballet and basic jazz dance steps.

About the Class

A fun and positive environment where kids can learn beginning ballet and basic jazz dance steps. Instruction will include warm up exercises, increased vocabulary and beginning mini ballet barre ballet positions, across the floor exercises, beginning turns and leaps and beginning choreography is taught at the end of class to different styles of music. Exercises will be modified or enhanced to the individual’s level. Class will be conducted in a positive and attentive environment and is designed to develop memorization, flexibility, and musicality. This class will help the student gain coordination and confidence to take into their daily lives. Small performing opportunities based upon a child’s interest but is not mandatory. Benefits include listening skills and concentration, increased coordination, and an understanding of movement, i.e. time, space, directions and balance, social skills and development of creativity.

title: Maija Peeples-Bright: Life is Just a Bowl of Terriers
description: Maija Peeples-Bright is one of the few women artists associated with Northern California's Funk Art movement of the late 1960s and '70s. Peeples-Bright's wildly inventive practice comprises robust compositions bustling with animals of all kinds-her "beasties" as she calls them-forming surreal, imaginative landscapes in audacious impasto. Her zoological arrangements represent alternate realities, infusing exuberance and a sense of the absurd into impossible scenarios. Highly distinctive, her textural creature patterns revel in the extreme, often taking up every inch of the canvas, multiplying her beasties into dizzying arrangements. Peeples-Bright invites us into a decadence-gone-wild world all her own. The exhibition will feature a retrospective look at Peeples-Bright's career, from her early days of the Funk Movement to today.

title: Cookbook Club
description: Are you interested in trying new recipes or just wanting to get out of your cooking comfort zone? A cookbook book club blends the joy of reading with the creativity of cooking and the warmth of sharing meals. Browse a selection of themed cookbooks every month, then prepare and share your meals for the next meeting! February: French cuisine - A visit to France through classic culinary techniques and fresh, modern influences. From slow braises and buttery sauces to inventive new flavors inspired by global ideas, French cooking blends tradition and creativity in every bite. Together, these methods make the cuisine vibrant, evolving, and undeniably yummy. March: Pie/Pi Month - We will celebrate pies in every form! From classic fruit pies and creamy custards to hearty pot pies, empanadas, samosas, turnovers, and galettes, this month honors the creativity and comfort of foods encased in a crust. It's a tasty reminder that nearly every culture has its own version of "pie," and every one of them is worth celebrating. April: SWANA - April marks Southwest Asian and North African \(SWANA\) Heritage Month and Arab American Heritage Month. SWANA is an inclusive, decolonial geographic term that represents the diverse communities of Southwest Asia and North Africa, including but not limited to Arab cultures. This month offers an opportunity to celebrate the region's rich culinary traditions-from aromatic spices and slow-cooked stews to vibrant mezze and sweets-highlighting food as a central expression of SWANA heritage. Plates, cutlery, and refreshments provided. Please be aware that dishes may contain or come into contact with common allergens, such as dairy, eggs, wheat, soybeans, tree nuts, peanuts, fish, or shellfish. If you are interested in attending please contact Kate at Sonoma Valley Library for more information. \(707\) 996-5217 kdeadder@sonomalibary.org

title: 2026 St. Pittie's Day Adoption Event
description: Come Meet Some Adorable and Ready-to-Adopt Pups On Saturday, March 14 from 1-3 PM, we're teaming up with the Humane Society of the North Bay to bring adoptable dogs of all breeds to our Coal Shed Brewery! Both Mugsy's Pet Supply and Powell Bros. Feed & Pet Supply will be here as well with shop items-perfect for new pet parents. Share a cuddly moment and pet some adoptable dogs on the waterfront. While we can't confirm beforehand exactly which dogs will be at the event, to view the adoptable HSNB dogs, visit hsnb.org/adoptable-dogs/ So, come by to meet them. Everyone will be happy you stopped by, and you may even meet your lucky match!

title: Second Saturday Cartoonists: Wahab Algarmi and Dean Stuart
description: Meet, watch, and talk to cartoonists Wahab Algarmi and Dean Stuart. Algarmi was a grant recipient for his comic work Town Force 1 and the Battle for East Oakland. His newest book, Almost Sunset, is listed on the School Library Journal’s best books of 2025. Stuart is an author and illustrator who has been published in Boom! comics, Darkhorse, and Penguin Random House. His most recent work is the newly published graphic novel Cassi and the House of Memories.

title: English Country Dance Marin
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or with friends, since we change partners after each dance. If you’ve ever done contra or square dancing, much of our dance vocabulary will be familiar to you since both of those styles evolved from English country dancing.

Admission is $15 for members of NBCDS and its affiliates.

title: Genealogy Class
description:
"Life in the Past Lane" a free genealogy class, will meet on the 2nd and 4th Saturday afternoons at the Lakeport Library. The class is conducted by the Lake County Genealogical Society \(LCGS\), is free and open to anyone interested in pursuing family history.  

 

Rose Davidson, president of the LCGS, will lead the class, which will explore an array of genealogy topics through class discussions, presentations, lessons and online webinars. At this first reintroduction meeting there is no need to bring anything except maybe a notebook / writing stuff. For more information about the class, email sleeperseeker@gmail.com. https://www.lakecountyca.gov/calendar.aspx?EID=2578

title: American Red Cross Adult and Pediatric First-Aid/CPR/AED Class (Blended)
description:
Learn how to save a life!

Focusing on recognizing and treating life-threatening situations in the critical minutes before 911 arrives, we will cover adult and infant CPR, AED use, handling choking as well as what to do in cases of severe bleeding, sudden illness, heart attack, stroke and more.

Focusing on recognizing and treating life-threatening situations in the critical minutes before 911 arrives, we will cover adult and infant CPR, AED use and handling choking, as well as what to do in cases of severe bleeding, sudden illness, heart attack, stroke and more.

We'll be using modern manikins and equipment in a small-group setting, and answering questions along the way. Your American Red Cross certificate will be valid for 2 years.

\(This community class event is held at a branch of Sonoma County Library. We thank them for their hospitality. They have requested that we mention that this program is neither sponsored by nor affiliated with the Sonoma County Library. Done!\)

Please get in touch with any questions!

title: Reclaim Your Calm A Harmonic Vagus Nerve Reset
description:
Reclaim Your Calm: Sound, Breath, & Flow

Join us for a unique somatic experience that uses the hypnotic, melodic resonance of the Rav Vast drum to bathe your nervous system in healing frequencies. Unlike traditional drumming, the Rav Vast creates a “sound forest” of overtones that helps the mind settle and the body soften. In this session, we integrate: Vibrational Sound Bath: The ethereal tones of the Rav Vast to lower your heart rate. Somatic Humming: To physically vibrate the vagus nerve from the inside out, syncing your breath with the drum. Flowing Qigong: Gentle, meditative movements to move “Qi” \(energy\) and release physical bracing in the body. Experience the shift from “survival mode” to “serenity.” Denise will guide you through: Qigong to open the body’s energy channels. Humming to stimulate your internal “calm” switch. A Rav Vast Sound Bath to wash away tension with beautiful, resonant harmonics. Come home to your body. Leave feeling grounded, peaceful, and renewed.

About Denise Aubin, MQT

Denise Aubin is a certified Medical Qigong Therapist \(MQT\) and the founder of Dance of Qigong. She discovered Qigong many years ago while traveling her own healing path and has a passion for assisting others on their healing journey back to love. Qigong is all about loving yourself and reconnecting with your joy. Denise loves teaching and empowering others in this ancient art form of self healing exercises. Denise received her degree from the International Institute of Medical Qigong and received certification as a Medical Qigong Therapist. Denise has been a teacher and healer for over thirty years.

title: Saturday Live Music at Spicy Vines feat. Honey Disposition
description:
Where unforgettable wines, live music, and good vibes come together.

Join us every Saturday from 3–6 PM at Spicy Vines for a tasting room experience like no other — where top local musicians from across the Bay Area meet our award-winning small-lot wines. From Rock & Roll and Blues to Jazz, Bluegrass, and Americana, each weekend brings new sounds and new reasons to raise a glass. 

Honey Disposition Rooted in friendship and a shared passion for original songwriting, Honey Disposition blends folk, jazz, rock, and pop into a signature sound that’s heartfelt, catchy, and full of soul. 

Details

No cover. Walk-ins are welcome, but this event tends to fill up quickly, so we recommend making a reservation. We are dog and family friendly!

title: Sea Chantey and Songs of the Sea Singalong
description: Please note the change of venue and time from our previous sing-alongs.\* Avast, ye saucy sailors! Come brave the perilous waters of Point Reyes and join us for a rollicking good time at Point Reyes National Seashore's Sea Chantey Sing-along at the Ken Patrick Visitor Center. Please bring your arsenal of sea chanteys, any musical instruments you'd like, and a willingness to laugh a lot!  It's so much fun when audience members lead shanties for the rest of us to join in on the chorus, and leading a song is encouraged! If you'd like to find a song \(or songs\) to share, I highly recommend setting one of your online music services to a sea chantey radio station and hear what's out there! Practice lyrics! Teach the chorus to our audience at the singalong! Our format is informal, mellow, and supportive, so if you've been wanting to push your comfort edge and lead a song, this is a perfect low-pressure opportunity. If you'd rather just singalong with the chantey choruses, that's fine too. We always have a great time no matter what. This free event is for chantey singers of all levels. Due to the nature of sea chanteys, there may be lyrics that are not appropriate for children. Reservations not required. To be added to the notification email list for future singalong dates, please email pore\_info@nps.gov For more information, please visit: nps.gov/pore/planyourvisit/calendar.htm

description:
Join Spreckels Theatre Education Program this winter for our 8 week teen production class of Between the Lines Jr, for ages 13-17.

 An outsider in a new town and a new school, Delilah seeks comfort in the pages of her favorite book, where she feels heard and understood by the handsome Prince Oliver. But as the lines between fantasy and reality begin to blur in extraordinary ways, Delilah realizes her dreams in the real world are worth fighting for.

title: Catering to the Car: A Retro Road Trip Down Petaluma’s Main Street
description: Hollie's 616 PBS Credit Paul Barber Collection Americana Reimagined at its best, this presentation will step into Petaluma’s living history with historian Katherine Rinehart as she shares stories behind some of Petaluma’s classic examples of roadside architecture. Discover how, through adaptive reuse, these properties reveal the economic and social trends that shaped the city’s past while continuing to serve the needs of today. $15 members; $20 non-members Get tickets #block-7ac69afb6caf81033ffe {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Katherine J. Rinehart is a consulting historian specializing in house history documentation, archival organization, research, and exhibit development. She holds an MA in History from Sonoma State University and recently founded the Petaluma Signs Project,  dedicated to preserving historic signs that enrich our unique identity. Previously, Katherine managed the Sonoma County History & Genealogy Library. Her current clients include Sonoma State University Library and the Tomales Regional History Center. Katherine is an official Petaluma Good Egg, an annual honor celebrating individuals who advance the story of the city's unique past. She is the author of Petaluma: A History in Architecture and co-authored The Petaluma River: A Sketch Journal and Brief Historical Overview and the Petaluma Vintage Signs Guidebook.
source: North Bay Bohemian, Petaluma Arts Center

title: Acoustic Saturday at Adobe Road Winery featuring Jeremy Gorman
location: Adobe Road Winery,6 Petaluma Boulevard North, Petaluma, CA 94952, USA
description:
Join us for a special Saturday acoustic live music set at Adobe Road Winery in downtown Petaluma. Sip on award-winning wines while enjoying talented local musicians in a warm and inviting atmosphere. Choose from wines by the glass or bottle to discover new favorites. Reserve your spot on Tock https://www.exploretock.com/adoberoadwines/ and make it a memorable night of great music, fine wine, and good company!

About Jeremy Gorman

For San Francisco Bay Area multi-instrumentalist, Jeremy Gorman, music has been nothing short of a lifetime journey of self-expression and self-realization. Currently performing as a solo artist, Jeremy has collaborated with many bands around the North Bay area, including a project titled spaceMüs with daughter, Blair Gorman.

With a deep knowledge of music theory, along with a vast range of musical tastes, Jeremy creates story-driven songs that capture ideas of hope and gratitude, and of his own personal life’s journey.

FLAMENCO! LIVE! ﻿was envisioned, conceived, and has thrived since 2002 in Sonoma County, California, now celebrating 24 years! Presenting the collaborative efforts of the Bay Area's finest Flamenco Singers, Guitarists and Dancers, and supporting local Flamenco students, dreamers, and talents, our goal has been to present top-notch performances. Phoebe Vernier "La Fibi" has brought decades of artistry and energy to the public of Sonoma County for the purpose of sharing the music, song and dance, the treasure of Southern Spain. Whether in the formal theater or in a casual setting, the respect, purity, and integrity of this traditional, yet living and evolving art form is always foremost.

Each event brings vibrant demonstrations of unusual dishes, to set up a sensational atmosphere for a next-level experience you would be hard-pressed to find anywhere else. In fact, the pop-up locations are not released until a week before each event. Beware, \(M\)EAT CARNIVAL isn't for everyone, so if getting your fingers dirty with exotic flavors and eating al fresco is your idea of fine dining, then this should be on your culinary bucket list. This one-of-a-kind experience is inspired by the flavors of the Mediterranean. Each pop-up event brings our team of chefs, each showcasing our interpretation of Medi-American cuisine cooked over a live fire. Explore the space, discovering something new around every corner - no two dishes are alike and no two \(M\)EAT CARNIVALS are exactly the same.

title: Outback Presents Whitney Cummings: The Big Baby Tour
location: Luther Burbank Center for the Arts
description:
Back to All Shows

Outback Presents 

Whitney Cummings: The Big Baby Tour

Saturday, March 14, 2026

7:00 pm

BUY TICKETS 

Whitney Cummings is a comedian, actor, writer, producer, director, and host of the hit podcast Good for You. Best known for creating and starring in the NBC series Whitney and co-creating the syndicated, Emmy-nominated CBS comedy 2 Broke Girls, she has become a leading voice in modern comedy. Cummings has released six stand-up specials, including Money Shot \(2010\), I Love You \(2014\), I’m Your Girlfriend \(2016\), Can I Touch It? \(2019\), Jokes \(2022\), and her latest, Mouthy, which debuted on OnlyFans in November 2023. She has also appeared in numerous television series and films, such as Good Mourning, Studio 666, How It Ends, and FOX’s Accused, further solidifying her presence on both the big and small screens. 

Beyond performing, Cummings is a successful author and filmmaker. In 2017, she published her memoir, I’m Fine… And Other Lies, and made her directorial debut with the feature adaptation of Louann Brizendine’s bestseller, The Female Brain. She continues to create, produce, and perform across platforms, bringing her sharp humor and fearless perspective to audiences worldwide. She currently lives in Los Angeles.

Post-Show Meet & Greet & Photo with Whitney Cummings.

Meet & Greet ticket DOES NOT include a seat for the show.

A separate show ticket MUST be purchased to attend Meet & Greet.

title: San Rafael Contra Dance
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
Contra Dance is fun, quick to learn, and great exercise. Every dance night has a live band, caller, and a quick start class for first timers.
In contra dance, you will dance with your partner but also with other couples as you progress down the line. The figures for each dance are done in a repeated sequence after a brief walk through by the caller. Each dance may last 10 – 12 minutes and then we encourage you to switch partners as the night goes on.
What to wear: Comfortable shoes that will enable you to pivot and slide on the wood floor.
When to arrive: If this is your first time, come 30 minutes early to check in, join the quick start class, and get you ready for the first dance.
How hard to learn? Most newcomers will find that they can get dancing right away as the figures are quite simple.
Come as a group, couple, or single! Our dance is inclusive to all ages and genders.
Cost: *Free to first-time dancers*. $13 members, $15 non-members, $8 student or limited income.

title: The Savannah Sipping Society; a Jones Hope Wooten Comedy
description:
**Four Southern women turn life’s curve balls into an uproarious, heartwarming journey of friendship, laughter, and fresh starts.**

In this delightful, laugh-a-minute comedy, four unique Southern women, all needing to escape the sameness of their day-to-day routines, are drawn together by Fate—and an impromptu happy hour—and decide it’s high time to reclaim the enthusiasm for life they’ve lost through the years. Over the course of six months, filled with laughter, hilarious misadventures, and the occasional liquid refreshment, these middle-aged women successfully bond and find the confidence to jump start their new lives. Together, they discover lasting friendships and a renewed determination to live in the moment—and most importantly, realize it’s never too late to make new old friends.

title: Marjorie Prime
description:
**Artificial Remembrance: The Intersection of Technology and Memory**

***“Jordan Harrison’s gently uncanny play imagines a future solution for a person in mourning: the recreation of someone you love as an artificial intelligence.”* —Kate Wyver, The Guardian**

**In *Marjorie Prime*, playwright Jordan Harrison delves into the intricate dance between human memory and artificial intelligence. Set in the not-so-distant future, the narrative centers on 85-year-old Marjorie, whose mind is gradually succumbing to dementia. To combat her fading recollections, her family introduces her to a “Prime”—a lifelike holographic projection of her late husband, Walter, programmed to retell the stories of their shared past. As Marjorie interacts with Walter Prime, the lines between authentic memories and reconstructed narratives blur, prompting profound questions about identity, the reliability of our recollections, and the extent to which technology can replicate the essence of human connection. This thought-provoking drama challenges audiences to consider what we choose to remember, what we wish to forget, and how the digital age reshapes the very fabric of our personal histories.**

***Marjorie Prime* has garnered critical acclaim for its insightful exploration and profound commentary on the human condition in an era where technology increasingly intertwines with our daily lives.**

Thursdays, Fridays & Saturdays @ 7:30pm, Saturdays and Sundays at 2:00pm Run Time: One hour, 20 mins including intermission

Thursdays Adult $33 Fri/Sat Eve Adult $39.95; Senior $36.95; Under 18 $26.95 Sat/Sun Matinee Adult $47.95; Senior $44.95 \*All prices include fees (707) 523-4185 **boxoffice@6thstreetplayhouse.com**

Their shared past ignites a firestorm of passion, leading them to abandon their new partners and escape together to Paris. This witty and chaotic escape reignites a turbulent romance filled with both adoration and dramatic clashes, as their spurned spouses, Victor and Sibyl, give chase. title: Charlie Hunter Trio
description: Charlie Hunter \(born May 23, 1967\) is an American guitarist, composer and bandleader.  First coming to prominence in the early '90s Hunter has since become one of the leading names in jazz, recording 17 albums. Hunter is noted for playing custom-made seven and eight-string guitars, on which he simultaneously plays basslines, rhythm guitar, and solos. Critic Sean Westergaard describes Hunter's innovative guitar technique as "mind-boggling ... he's an agile improviser with an ear for great tone, and always has excellent players alongside him in order to make great music, not to show off."
